pqc_capability_description: First quantum-safe fork of Bitcoin. Testnet replaces ECDSA with NIST-standardized ML-DSA signatures to protect against quantum threats. Increased block size to 64 MiB to accommodate larger PQC signatures (38-72x larger than ECDSA). Full transaction lifecycle with quantum-resistant cryptography. Protects ~6.26M BTC in addresses with exposed public keys against Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks.
product_brief: First quantum-safe Bitcoin fork demonstrating ML-DSA feasibility for blockchain signatures. Open permissionless testnet for miners developers researchers and users.

pqc_capability_description: First post-quantum testnet for Solana blockchain developed in collaboration with Solana Foundation. Replaces EdDSA signatures with NIST-standardized ML-DSA demonstrating quantum-resistant transactions are practical on high-throughput blockchain architectures (65000+ TPS). Open-sourced testnet with readiness assessments and migration test environments. $20M Series A funding announced January 2026.
product_brief: Demonstrates high-throughput blockchains can successfully implement post-quantum signatures. Critical for Web3 and decentralized finance PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Post-quantum semiconductor-based trust anchors deployed in U.S. systems. QS7001 and QVault-TPM post-quantum secure processors incorporating quantum-resistant algorithms and hardware roots of trust. Protection against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks. Millions of devices deployed worldwide. Joint venture with Kaynes SemiCon in India for PQC chip manufacturing.
product_brief: Post-quantum semiconductor chips with embedded quantum-resistant cryptography. First deployments in U.S. government and critical infrastructure.

pqc_capability_description: Hardware-based plug-and-play post-quantum encryption platform. NSA CNSA 2.0 compliant protocol-agnostic solution working with legacy and modern networks. Features one-way data diode with NIST-approved PQC algorithms including ML-KEM. Tested by U.S. military branches with SpaceX deployment. Secures data in transit without software rewrites or PKI dependencies.
product_brief: Hardware-based quantum-resistant encryption for high-security environments. Deployed across U.S. military branches and SpaceX missions.

pqc_capability_description: First end-to-end cryptographic security platform for quantum era. Works across cloud on-premise and edge environments. Includes QuEverywhere for mobile PQC.

pqc_capability_description: Platform for automated discovery and inventory of cryptographic assets. Deployed in U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) CIO systems. HQC algorithm contributor selected by NIST.

Not standard Classic McEliece. Powers Hitachi DoMobile Ver.5 remote access system in Japan. Developing Quantum AI Wrapper (QAW) for securing AI data with Full Homomorphic Encryption and Quantum Crypto Wrapper (QCW) for blockchain quantum-safety.
product_brief: NIST-compliant quantum-safe engine enabling partners to deploy PQC. Focus on agile deployment and AI/blockchain security.

pqc_capability_description: Implements HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic) and ML-KEM for .NET platform. Provides quantum-resistant key exchange mechanisms with good performance characteristics on .NET runtime.

product_brief: Comprehensive Java implementation of cryptographic algorithms. Supports JCE/JCA provider lightweight API PGP S/MIME DTLS TLS OCSP TSP CMP and certificate generation.

pqc_capability_description: Full support for NIST-standardized PQC algorithms including ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism) for key exchange, ML-DSA (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm) for signatures, and SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Algorithm). Enables quantum-safe TLS 1.3 connections with hybrid mode support.
product_brief: Robust commercial-grade TLS/SSL and crypto library. Implements TLS 1.3 DTLS 1.3 and QUIC protocols. Reference implementation for cryptographic operations used by majority of HTTPS websites.

Project restructured into language-specific repos (tink-java, tink-go, etc.).

pqc_capability_description: Supports hybrid post-quantum key exchange in TLS 1.3 using ML-KEM or FrodoKEM. Includes post-quantum signature schemes: ML-DSA (Dilithium), SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+), HSS/LMS, XMSS. Includes post-quantum key encapsulation schemes: ML-KEM (Kyber), FrodoKEM, Classic McEliece.

pqc_capability_description: FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. Supports hybrid mode (classical + PQC) and includes CryptoComply PQ TLS for quantum-safe data in transit. Drop-in OpenSSL compatibility. CryptoComply Go v4.0 adds all NIST PQC algorithms (ML-KEM FIPS 203 ML-DSA FIPS 204 SLH-DSA FIPS 205). PQC capabilities rolled out across Core v3.5 (OpenSSL-based) Mobile v3.5 Java v4 and Go v4.

pqc_capability_description: Version 8.6 enables quantum-safe TLS 1.3 handshake using hybrid ECDHE-MLKEM key agreement based on X25519MLKEM768. ML-KEM is a standardized FIPS-203 algorithm for key encapsulation, preventing session key decryption by quantum computers.
product_brief: SAP's proprietary cryptographic library now enabling PQC for SAP applications and secure communications.

pqc_capability_description: Comprehensive SDK for PQC integration. Features PQCryptoLib-Core (FIPS 140-3 certified) and PQCryptoLib-Soft. Supports mobile, server, and embedded.
product_brief: PQShield PQCryptoLib-SDK development kit (also marketed as PQSDK). High-assurance post-quantum cryptography SDK for enterprise and embedded security. Note: product is formally named PQCryptoLib-SDK.

pqc_capability_description: Native ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 since Go 1.24 (Feb 2025). Hybrid post-quantum TLS enabled by default since Go 1.24 combining classical elliptic-curve cryptography with ML-KEM. Go 1.26 adds crypto/hpke and crypto/mlkem/mlkemtest packages with derandomized encapsulation for known-answer tests. FIPS 203 compliant.
product_brief: Standard library implementation of NIST FIPS 203 key encapsulation. Hybrid PQC TLS default since Go 1.24; Go 1.26 adds crypto/hpke and mlkemtest.

pqc_capability_description: Implements ML-KEM (formerly Kyber), a post-quantum secure key encapsulation mechanism standardized by NIST in FIPS 203. Supports three security levels: MlKem512 (128-bit), MlKem768 (192-bit), and MlKem1024 (256-bit). Implementation is pure Rust, version 0.2.3. Security warning states it has never been independently audited.
product_brief: Pure Rust ML-KEM implementation standardizing on FIPS 203.

pqc_capability_description: Implements ML-DSA (formerly CRYSTALS-Dilithium) per FIPS 204 final. Supports parameter sets MlDsa44 (Category 2), MlDsa65 (Category 3), and MlDsa87 (Category 5). Implementation is pure Rust, supports `no_std`, and includes key generation, signing, and verification. Warning: Never independently audited; use at own risk.
product_brief: Pure Rust ML-DSA implementation compliant with FIPS 204.

pqc_capability_description: Implements Stateless Hash-based Digital Signature Algorithm (SLH-DSA) based on SPHINCS+, finalized in NIST FIPS-205. Supports 12 parameter sets (SHA2 and SHAKE variants at L1, L3, L5 security). Signatures range from 7KB to 50KB. Implementation is not independently audited; requires Rust 1.75+.
product_brief: Pure Rust SLH-DSA implementation compliant with FIPS 205.

pqc_capability_description: Provides Rust bindings to C implementations of cryptographic algorithms from the NIST competition, generated from the PQClean project. Supports variants controlled by `implementations.yaml`. Maturity is indicated by repository activity and documentation on docs.rs.
product_brief: Rust wrapper around PQClean providing FFI bindings to reference implementations.

pqc_capability_description: Post-quantum secure with a 128-bit security level. Supports hybridization using INDCPA-KYBER (INDCPA version of NIST standard Kyber). Data encrypted with AES-GCM 256-bit. Uses Curve25519 for classic coordinates and INDCPA-Kyber for hybridized coordinates.
product_brief: Attribute-based encryption with quantum resistance, compliant with ETSI TS 104 015.

pqc_capability_description: Library contains implementations of post-quantum key-encapsulation mechanisms and signature schemes targeting ARM Cortex-M4. Results show measurable but small performance impact on bandwidth and latency, confirming technical feasibility without inherent issues.

pqc_capability_description: Detects PQC and hybrid algorithm usage in live network traffic and PCAP captures. TLS 1.3 detection: X25519Kyber768 (Chrome 0x11ec), X25519Kyber768Draft00 (Cloudflare 0xfe31/0x6399), NIST ML-KEM drafts (0x2001-0x2010) via custom ClientHello/ServerHello supported_groups and key_share extension parser. QUIC detection via TLS-in-QUIC handshake parsing. IKE/IPsec via nDPI classification. PQC flags bitmask tracks KEM presence, signature presence, hybrid negotiation, offered-only, and session resumption states. No nDPI patches required for SSH/TLS PQC extraction.
product_brief: Privacy-preserving passive network analyzer detecting PQC algorithm usage in TLS; SSH; and QUIC traffic. Analyzes flow metadata only (no payload storage). Enterprise certificate management portal for TLS S/MIME and code signing certificates. PQC hybrid certificate issuance available; underpins the DigiCert Trust Lifecycle Manager platform for enterprise quantum-safe PKI migration.

Enterprise-grade unified CLM platform with PQC hybrid certificate support. Consolidates PKI, device trust, document signing, and software signing under one management plane. Critical migration platform for organizations with large certificate inventories.

Cloud IAM and MFA platform requiring PQC migration for authentication token signing and TLS session key establishment. Leverages Entrust PKI and nShield HSM infrastructure for eventual quantum-safe credential lifecycle management.

pqc_capability_description: Crypto4A QxEDGE is a purpose-built edge security appliance delivering PQC-native HSM capabilities for IoT, OT, and edge computing environments. Supports ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, XMSS, and LMS. Designed for deployment in resource-constrained and air-gapped edge networks.
product_brief: Edge PQC security appliance providing HSM capabilities for IoT and OT environments. Full NIST PQC algorithm suite including stateful hash-based signatures (XMSS/LMS) for secure boot and firmware signing at the network edge.
Centralized management and analytics platform for FortiGate NGFW deployments. PQC roadmap status unknown — no documentation available.
Centralized authentication, certificate management, and SSO appliance for Fortinet Security Fabric. PQC roadmap status unknown — no documentation available.

pqc_capability_description: IBM z/OS ICSF (Integrated Cryptographic Service Facility) provides quantum-safe cryptography on IBM Z hardware (z16+). Supports ML-KEM and ML-DSA via z/OS V2.5+ with hardware acceleration through the IBM Crypto Express 8S adapter. Provides quantum-safe API functions for application programs, migrating from Dilithium/Kyber to ML-DSA/ML-KEM per NIST FIPS 203/204. Part of IBM's quantum-safe z/OS infrastructure.
product_brief: Mainframe cryptographic services with hardware-accelerated PQC via IBM z16 CPACF chip. On-chip ML-KEM and ML-DSA acceleration without performance penalty. Critical for financial institutions running transaction processing workloads on IBM Z infrastructure.

Enterprise relational database requiring quantum-safe TLS migration for client connections and key wrapping upgrades for data-at-rest encryption. Part of IBM Quantum Safe data protection roadmap targeting financial and government data stores.

Enterprise messaging middleware for financial transaction processing. TLS channel security between queue managers requires quantum-safe key exchange migration. Part of IBM Quantum Safe messaging infrastructure roadmap for SWIFT and financial clearing networks.

Cloud-delivered SASE platform with TLS inspection and IPsec requiring quantum-safe migration. SASE edge points terminate large volumes of TLS traffic making ML-KEM hybrid deployment critical for preventing harvest-now-decrypt-later against enterprise traffic.

Enterprise CLM platform for automated certificate lifecycle management. Separate from GlobalSign Digital Signing Service; Atlas focuses on enterprise TLS and PKI automation. PQC hybrid certificate support expected as root CA infrastructure is upgraded.

pqc_capability_description: Sectigo Certificate Manager (SCM) is the enterprise CLM platform for TLS, S/MIME, code signing, and IoT certificates. PQC hybrid certificate issuance planned as Sectigo CA infrastructure is upgraded to support ML-DSA. Note: may overlap with Sectigo Certificate Manager product listing — verify naming.
product_brief: Enterprise CLM platform for certificate automation and lifecycle management. PQC hybrid certificate support roadmapped. Sectigo is one of the world's largest commercial CAs by volume, making its PQC migration timeline significant for the broader internet ecosystem.

pqc_capability_description: FortiWeb 8.0.3 (December 2025) introduces PQC in TLS 1.3 via OpenSSL 3.5: ML-KEM key exchange groups (mlkem512/mlkem768/mlkem1024) plus hybrid ECC-PQC modes and ML-DSA signature algorithms. CLI-configurable for both client-side server policies and server pool connections.
product_brief: Fortinet FortiWeb is an enterprise Web Application Firewall (WAF). Version 8.0.3 introduced full PQC TLS 1.3 support via OpenSSL 3.5: ML-KEM key exchange (mlkem512/768/1024) hybrid ECC-PQC and ML-DSA certificates — the first Fortinet product with GA PQC support.

pqc_capability_description: Supports Post Quantum Cryptography algorithms: Crystal Kyber (KEM) and Crystal Dilithium (Signature). Features a dedicated ad-X4 hardware cryptographic accelerator for fast encryption/authentication including PQC acceleration.

Provides latest NIST-standardized post-quantum and PQ/T Hybrid algorithms in a software environment. Optimized for crypto-agility and FIPS-compliant PQ/T Hybrid solutions to protect against 'Harvest Now Decrypt Later' attacks. Supports smooth transition to quantum resistance.

The product helps organizations "prepare for PQC readiness," "assess post-quantum cryptography risk," and "assess the efficiency and feasibility of PQC algorithms." It enables a "quantum-resilient algorithm posture" but does not explicitly state that it currently implements specific PQC algorithms in production.
ST33K Series: First PQC-Protected TPM Roadmap

pqc_support: Yes (based on Keccak; supports NIST standardized algorithms ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA)
pqc_capability_description: Hardware accelerator utilizing Keccak to offload intensive tasks from the main processor. Optimizes performance for quantum-resistant algorithms standardized by NIST: ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205). Designed for seamless transition without compromising speed.

The solution delivers quantum-safe Layer 1 encryption utilizing post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and post-quantum key exchange. It is approved by the German Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) for VS-NfD and EU/NATO-restricted data, protecting against harvest-now, decrypt-later attacks.

The product features enhanced security with encrypted communications using post-quantum TLS 1.3. It is part of a suite designed to help organizations map assets and prepare for PQC migration, but the probe itself primarily detects existing protocols rather than implementing PQC algorithms for data protection beyond its own management interface.
Citrix: Virtual Apps & Desktops Security Baseline

pqc_support: Yes (with details: Supports hybrid PQC key exchange using ML-KEM/FIPS 203 and Pre-shared Post-Quantum Keys (PPK) for IPsec).
pqc_capability_description: Provides real-time inline inspection of PQC traffic via hybrid PQC key exchange in TLS 1.2/1.3, auto-detecting ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and Open Quantum Safe groups alongside classical curves. Supports IPsec with Pre-shared Post-Quantum Keys (PPK) per RFC 8784 to protect IKE key derivation against quantum attacks without requiring full PQC algorithms in the exchange.

Supports ML-KEM based hybrid key exchange for transport layer security (TLS) 1.3. Available via "Post Quantum Cryptography Client to Edge" behavior for Enhanced TLS hostnames. Can be disabled if high latency or error rates occur. Limited availability; requires contacting Akamai representative.

The document states Keyfactor is a leader in "post-quantum readiness" and predicts vendors will release products with PQC capabilities in 2026. It mentions a "PQC Lab" sandbox for getting quantum-ready but does not explicitly state that the core production platform currently supports specific PQC algorithms or hybrid modes.

pqc_support: Planned (with timeline: Spring 2026 release focuses on readiness and visibility)
pqc_capability_description: The Spring 2026 release expands cryptographic visibility to support post-quantum cryptography (PQC) initiatives through enhanced agentless discovery of certificates and cipher usage. It enables planning for PQC readiness and modernization but does not explicitly state the implementation of specific PQC algorithms or production support yet.

pqc_support: Planned (with timeline: 2026 execution, standards maturity by end of 2026)
pqc_capability_description: The document is a prediction article, not a product specification. It states that 2026 will be the year of PQC execution with pilot rollouts and automation. It mentions NIST finalizing foundational standards in 2024 and expects formal definitions for PQC versions of major certificate types by end of 2026. No specific algorithms or implementation details for Sectigo products are provided.

The platform advances full-stack PQC readiness through expansion announced in February 2026. It integrates hardware security modules, PKI, certificate life-cycle management, discovery, and compliance governance to support enterprise quantum preparedness and crypto-agility. Specific algorithms are not listed.

This is an Internet-Draft proposing a TLS extension (`pq_cert_available`) to prevent downgrade attacks during PQC migration. It allows clients to cache server commitments to use PQC algorithms (specifically referencing ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and composites). It is not a software product but a protocol specification under development.

The document states that 2026 will be the year of execution for PQC, with enterprises pivoting to pilot rollouts and full automation. It mentions NIST finalizing foundational standards in 2024 and PQC appearing in TLS handshakes by 2026. Specific algorithms are not listed, but "quantum-safe key exchange" is noted as the first practical step.
NIST CAVP \#A7679: ML-KEM Hardware IP Core Validation
Production-ready, NIST-aligned hybrid post-quantum cryptography using X25519 + ML-KEM768. Provides forward-looking protection and backward compatibility. Available now for external-facing services.

Supports full suite of CNSA2.0 prescribed PQC algorithms including LMS, XMSS, ML-DSA, ML-KEM, and SLH-DSA. PQC services (XMSS/LMS, ML-DSA, ML-KEM) are available specifically in TDQ devices to address quantum threats.

The Trusted Security Foundation (TSF) provides PQC crypto-agile key management supporting the latest NIST Quantum Resistant Algorithms. The qConnect Quantum Safe Key Distributor enables quantum safe key distribution using NIST approved PQC algorithms, standard industry protocols, and supports hybrid QKD/PQC key delivery. Integration with PQShield quantum resistant algorithm implementations is described for TSF KMS and qClient SDK.
AWS: Post-Quantum TLS Support in AWS KMS and Certificate Manager

PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA) are available through CNG and Certificate functions for Windows Insiders and Linux. TLS 1.3 hybrid key exchange is enabled in SymCrypt-OpenSSL v1.9.0. Full transition of services planned by 2033, with early adoption starting 2029.

Supports hybrid post-quantum key establishment across all existing TLS security policies and a new TLS 1.3 only policy (TLS1.3_2025). PQC is automatically enabled for client-to-edge connections with no customer configuration required. Available in all edge locations at no additional charge.

PQC capabilities are previewed for Windows Insiders and Linux via SymCrypt updates. ML-KEM and ML-DSA are available through CNG. TLS hybrid key exchange is enabled in SymCrypt-OpenSSL 1.9.0. Full transition of services aims for completion by 2033, with early adoption starting by 2029.

Windscribe apps support post-quantum WireGuard out of the box using TLS 1.3 with the hybrid key exchange mechanism X25519MLKEM768 to share the PresharedKey. The PSK is rotated on each login. Validated with Wireshark.

NordVPN has rolled out post-quantum encryption (PQE) across all its VPN apps (Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Android TV, tvOS, Linux). The upgrade integrates quantum-resistant algorithms into the NordLynx protocol (based on WireGuard), complying with NIST standards. Initially deployed on Linux in September 2024 to collect performance data, it expanded to all platforms in 2025. PQE is enabled via a toggle switch in settings and activates automatically when connecting via NordLynx.

Supports analyzing real-world post-quantum TLS traffic using hybrid key agreement (X25519 + Kyber768 draft). Capable of decrypting sessions via embedded Decryption Secrets Blocks (DSB) in pcapng files. Mentions Cloudflare and Google server-side PQ KEX deployment and client support in Chrome 124 and Firefox 124/128.

Supports X25519MLKEM768 as the default post-quantum hybrid key agreement. Also supports X25519Kyber768Draft00 for specific version ranges (Edge 124-130, Firefox 124-131). Implementation relies on underlying libraries like BoringSSL or OpenSSL with PQC providers.

Payment HSM with crypto-agile architecture. Futurex CryptoHub general-purpose HSM has ML-DSA, ML-KEM, and SLH-DSA support with PCI HSM validation (June 2025) — currently the only PCI-HSM-validated option with PQC. Vectera Plus payment-specific firmware PQC status unclear. PCI PTS HSM v3 certified.
fips_validated: Yes (PCI PTS HSM v3 FIPS 140-2 L3)
product_brief: Payment HSM for transaction processing PIN management and key injection. Futurex general HSM platform already has PQC.

pqc_support: Yes (with details: CRYSTALS-KYBER, CRYSTALS-DILITHIUM, FALCON, SPHINCS+, BIKE, Classic McEliece, HQC, NTRU, FRODO, SABER, NTRULPrime, SNTRUPrime, Picnic)
pqc_capability_description: This is a concept study to run PQC algorithms using Bouncy Castle 1.72. It supports Public-key Encryption/KEMs (CRYSTALS-KYBER, BIKE, Classic McEliece, HQC, NTRU, FRODO, SABER, NTRULPrime, SNTRUPrime) and Digital Signatures (CRYSTALS-DILITHIUM, FALCON, SPHINCS+, Picnic). SIKE is excluded as broken; Rainbow is noted as not available in this version. The app generates key pairs, encodes keys to byte arrays, performs KEM key exchange/encryption/decryption, and signature sign/verify flows.

pqc_support: Yes (Limited availability for Ghost to Origin connections; General Availability planned for October 31, 2025)
pqc_capability_description: Akamai rolled out PQC support on June 30, 2025, for Ghost to Origin (G2O) connections using TLS 1.3 hybrid key exchange. The implementation uses the X25519MLKEM768 group (hybrid of X25519 and ML-KEM768). It is currently in limited availability with a planned general availability release on October 31, 2025, for all origin TLS connections.

Juniper SRX Series firewalls run Junos OS and provide enterprise-grade IPsec VPN, IPS, and application security. Junos 25.4R1 delivers PQC ML-DSA-87 image signatures for SRX Series. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 for VPN tunnels is on the Juniper roadmap targeting 2026. Juniper Networks is part of the ETSI QKD and NIST PQC working groups.
product_brief: Enterprise/carrier NGFW on Junos OS. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 planned for Junos 2026. Juniper active in ETSI QKD and NIST PQC working groups.

pqc_capability_description: Full PQC support via Q-safe firmware extension (v5.0+). Supports ML-KEM-512/768/1024, ML-DSA-44/65/87, XMSS, and LMS. Highest physical security at FIPS 140-2 Level 4. Supports custom PQC algorithm loading through CryptoServer SDK. Available in network and PCIe form factors.
